prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `ea9794b1`.
codex_response:
Rule: split the 10×10 input into four 5×5 quadrants, treat them as fixed-color layers (`4` top-left, `3` top-right, `9` bottom-left, `8` bottom-right), and overlay them into one 5×5 output with priority `3 > 9 > 8 > 4 > 0`. I used the summary hint because it matched the official examples exactly. No new shared primitive was needed.

The new task package is at [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_ea9794b1/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_ea9794b1/verifier.py#L4), and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_ea9794b1/generator.py#L179). The generator matches the family by sampling quadrant-local 5×5 monochrome masks with densities and fragmentation close to the official task, then composing the 10×10 input and deriving the output by the same priority overlay.

Verification passed:
- official examples: `7/7`
- generated examples: `1000/1000`
- visual sanity check: generated preview sheet is on-family relative to the originals

Artifacts are at [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/ea9794b1/task.json), [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/ea9794b1/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/ea9794b1/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/ea9794b1/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/ea9794b1/previews/preview_03.png).
